title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 12 12

questions.question: Pointing to a boy, Hridaan said, "He is the son of my wife's mother's only daughter." How is the boy related to Hridaan?
questions.explanation: Pointing to a boy, Hridaan said, "He is the son of my wife's mother's only daughter." ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30431/image003.png) That boy is the son of Hridaan.

questions.question: Six persons P, Q, R, S, T, and U have different ages (in years), viz., 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, and 35, but not necessarily in the same order. Q's age is a multiple of 6. R is younger than Q. The age of R is an even number. P is not immediate older than R. Only two persons are older than P. U is older than S and T. The age of T is an odd number. What is the sum of the ages of R and U?
questions.explanation: Six persons P, Q, R, S, T, and U have different ages (in years), viz., 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, and 35, but not necessarily in the same order. Q's age is a multiple of 6. R is younger than Q. The age of R is an even number. Case 1Case 2 Ages (in years) Persons Persons 10R 15 20R 25 30QQ 35 Only two persons are older than P. P is not immediate older than R. Thus, case 2 is invalid. Case 1 Ages (in years) Persons 10R 15 20 25P 30Q 35 U is older than S and T. The age of T is an odd number. Therefore, we get: Ages (in years) Persons 10R 15T 20S 25P 30Q 35U The sum of the ages of R and U = 10+35 = 45 years.

questions.question: In a Teacher Training series held daily from 26th Apr to 3rd May 2023, the sessions were scheduled as follows: Sonepat - 26th Apr 2023, Lingsugur - 27th Apr 2023, Nainital - ?, Khandwa - ?, Rajsamand - 30th Apr 2023, Porbandar - 1st May 2023, Mahbubnagar - 2nd May 2023, Tirupati - 3rd May 2023. It is known that Nainital was held exactly 2 days before Rajsamand, and Khandwa was held immediately after Nainital. How many days after the session in Lingsugur did the session in Khandwa occur?
questions.explanation: Rajsamand was on 30th Apr 2023. So, Nainital was 2 days before 30th Apr, i.e., 28th Apr 2023. Khandwa was immediately after 28th Apr, so Khandwa was on 29th Apr 2023. Lingsugur was on 27th Apr 2023. From 27th Apr to 29th Apr = 2 days.

questions.question: Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for each item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. Karan finds a wallet on the road with cash, an ID card, and no phone number. He is in a hurry but can spare 10 minutes. What is the most appropriate action?
options: Keep cash and throw the wallet away
Submit it at the nearest police desk
Post a wallet photo on social media
Leave it where it was found
questions.explanation: The goal is to return the wallet safely and responsibly with proof of handover. Option B uses an official channel where the ID details can help locate the owner. It also protects Karan from suspicion because there is a clear record of submission. Option A is dishonest and harms the owner. Option C can expose the owner's personal details and still may not reach them safely. Option D risks someone else taking it and the owner losing everything.

questions.question: Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for each item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. A municipal team must decide how to repair a damaged road used by an ambulance route. Option A is full resurfacing, which is durable but will take 18 days with major blockage. Option B is a quick patchwork that opens in 2 days but may need repair again after heavy rain. The monsoon is expected within 3 weeks, and traffic cannot be blocked for long right now. Which response is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: The route is critical, so a long blockage is risky for emergency movement. Option A Monsoon is close, so immediate full resurfacing with an 18-day blockage is impractical now. Option B ignores long-term durability and increases repeated breakdown risk. Option C restores usability quickly and plans durable work at a safer time window. Option D delays and keeps the ambulance route unsafe.

questions.question: Radha wanted to rent a book from the library. She started from her house and went 11km in the east direction and then turned left. She then moved 7km and turned right and moved 5km. Then she went 5km south to reach the library. In which direction is the library with respect to her house?
questions.explanation: She started from her house and went 11km in the east direction and then turned left. She then moved 7km and turned right and moved 5km. Then she went 5km south to reach the library. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p17879/image008.jpg) So, the library is in northeast direction with respect to the house.

questions.question: Every month a person saves ₹1600 more than the savings of his previous month. If he saves a total of ₹360000 from January 2015 to December 2015, how much did he save in January 2015?
questions.explanation: Clearly, it's a question of A.P. where n = 12, Sₙ= 360000, d =1600. Let a be the savings of January's month Sₙ = n/2 x (2a + (n-1)d => 6(2a + 11 x 1600) = 360000 => 2a + 17600 = 60000 => 2a = 42400 => a = ₹21200

Find the next number in the series. 950, 901, 865, 840, 824, ?

questions.question: Which number set is different? (12, 60, 20), (21, 105, 35), (36, 180, 60), (19, 95, 25)
questions.explanation: Here, the logic is: Option (a) (12, 60, 20) 12*5 = 60, 60 ÷ 3 = 20 Option (b) (21, 105, 35) 21*5 = 105, 105 ÷ 3 = 35 Option (c) (36, 180, 60) 36*5 = 180, 180 ÷ 3 = 60 Option (d) (19, 95, 25) 19*5 = 95, 95 ÷ 3 = 31.6 Thus, the number set given in option (d) is different.

questions.question: If '∑' means '÷', '∆' means '+', '∂' means '-' and '∞' means ' X ', then find the value of the following expression. 52 ∆ (14² ∑ √49) ∞ 15 ∂ 289 = ?
√A = 84 - 69 and B² - 254 = 371, and P ^ Q = (A X B) ÷ 25, then what is P ^ Q?
In each cluster of five letters, three clusters have an integer mean of alphabetical positions; one does not. Identify the odd cluster.
Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to replace the * signs and balance the given equation. 216 * 6 * 3 * 2 = 24
questions.explanation: Using the operators from option (A), the equation becomes 216 ÷ 6 ÷ 3 X 2 = 24. Evaluating from left to right: • 216 ÷ 6 = 36 • 36 ÷ 3 = 12 • 12 X 2 = 24 The equation is balanced.

questions.question: A number has been denoted to each of the given letters. Select the option from the following four possible arrangements of these numbers that forms a meaningful word. S = 1, C = 2, R = 3, E = 4, P = 5, S = 6, O = 7
questions.explanation: The possible words from the given options are: Option (a) 1, 6, 2, 4, 3, 5, 7 = SSCERPO, which is not a meaningful word. Option (b) 5, 3, 7, 2, 4, 6, 6 = PROCESS, which is a meaningful word. Option (c) 7, 5, 4, 2, 1, 6, 3 = OPECSSR, which is not a meaningful word. Option (d) 3, 6, 1, 7, 4, 5, 2 = RSSOEPC, which is not a meaningful word.

questions.question: Which of the words among the given options CANNOT be formed using the letters from the following sentence? Please update software.
questions.explanation: The letter 'L' appears only once in the sentence 'Please update software'. The word 'Stroll' contains 'L' twice. So, 'Stroll' cannot be formed using the letters of the sentence.

questions.question: Assuming that the given statement is true, find out which of the four alternative(s) is definitely true. Statement: The censor board certifies films before it is shown in theaters. A. All movies which are shown in theaters are not rejected by the censor board. B. Some movies are not rejected by the censor board but not shown in theaters. C. All movies are not shown in the theaters.
questions.explanation: The statements states that, 'the censor board certifies films before it is shown in theaters'. It means if the movie is not certified by the censor board, then it is not shown in the theaters. (A) All movies which are shown in theaters are not rejected by the censor board: The movies which are rejected by the censor board are not shown in the theaters and vice versa. So, (A) is true. (B) Some movies are not rejected by the censor board but not shown in theaters: Some certified movies by the censor board may not be shown in theaters. It depends on the theater's owner. So, we cannot definitely state if it is shown or not in theaters. So, (B) is false. (C) All movies are not shown in the theaters: We cannot definite say that all movies are not shown in theaters. So, (C) is false.

questions.question: If SNR is coded as SPY ONE JAR, then what code can be considered for TWP?
questions.explanation: SNR is coded as SPY ONE JAR Here, the pattern is: S = the first letter of the word SPY is 'S'. N = the second letter of the word ONE is 'N'. R = the third letter of the word JAR is 'R'. Similarly, only in option (b): T = the first letter of the word TAJ is 'T'. W = the second letter of the word OWN is 'W'. P = the third letter of the word ZIP is 'P'.

questions.question: A family has eight members. B is the son of P while P and T are the sister-in-laws of U. Y is the mother of U, who is unmarried. C is the son of I. B is the nephew of W. Which of the following statements is/are correct? I. B is the son of T. II. I is the father of U. III. U is the sister-in-law of T.
questions.explanation: A family has eight members. B is the son of P while P and T are the sister-in-laws of U. Y is the mother of U, who is unmarried. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22277/image017.jpg) C is the son of I. B is the nephew of W. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22277/image018.jpg) Thus, only II is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following order of numbers will make the equation below correct? 4 + 12 x 5 ÷ 15 - 30 = 21
questions.explanation: Checking the interchanges by each option: LHS = 12 + 4 x 30 ÷ 5 - 15 = 12 + 4 x 6 - 15 = 12 + 24 - 15 = 21 So, (a) is correct. LHS = 30 + 15 x 12 ÷ 4 - 5 = 30 + 15 x 3 - 5 = 30 + 45 - 5 = 70 So, (b) is not correct. LHS = 30 + 4 x 15 ÷ 5 - 12 = 30 + 4 x 3 - 12 = 30 + 12 - 12 = 30 So, (c) is not correct. LHS = 12 + 4 x 30 ÷ 15 - 5 = 12 + 4 x 2 - 5 = 12 + 8 - 5 = 15 So, (d) is not correct.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. YAC, BEH, EIM, ? OUA
questions.explanation: The middle letters are the vowels in the alphabetical order. Here, the pattern is: Y+2=A and A+2=C B+3=E and E+3=H E+4=I and I+4=M O+6=U and U+6=A So, the fourth term will be: J+5=O and O+5=T

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 186, 371, 741, 1481, ?, 5921, 11841
Which of the following is not a feature of federalism in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Single citizenship is NOT a feature of federalism in the Indian Constitution. In fact, it is a unitary feature that goes against the typical federal model. Unlike the U.S. or Australia, there is no concept of dual citizenship (national + state). Though the Indian Constitution has some federal features and envisages a dual polity, it provides for only a single citizenship, that is, Indian. In India, all citizens, regardless of the state of residence, enjoy the same political and civil rights of citizenship throughout the country. In the case of USA, the people of USA have dual citizenships of both the country as well as the states in which they are born. Classical federal systems like the United States, Switzerland, and Australia typically have dual citizenship (national and state citizenship).

questions.question: Arrange the following dynasties of Magadha in correct chronological order: A. Mauryan dynasty B. Haryanka dynasty C. Nanda dynasty D. Shishunaga dynasty
questions.explanation: Haryanka → Shishunaga → Nanda → Maurya Haryanka dynasty - earliest (Bimbisara, Ajatashatru) Shishunaga dynasty - succeeded Haryankas Nanda dynasty - followed Shishunagas Mauryan dynasty - founded by Chandragupta Maurya after Nandas

questions.question: Simranpreet Kaur Brar won the gold medal in which of the following events at the ISSF World Cup Final 2025 held in Doha?
questions.explanation: Simranpreet Kaur Brar is a rising star in Indian shooting who specializes in the 25m Pistol event. In the 2025 ISSF World Cup Final in Doha, she delivered a clinical performance to clinch the gold medal, Unlike the 10m event (which uses air pellets), the 25m event uses .22 caliber ammunition and involves both "precision" and "rapid-fire" stages. Further Insights ISSF = International Shooting Sport Federation. World Cup Finals include only top performers of the season. India has emerged as a global shooting powerhouse in recent years. Shooting contributes significantly to India's international medal tally. Pistol, rifle, and shotgun are the three main shooting categories.

questions.question: Read the following statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct option: Assertion (A): The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) aims to provide clean cooking fuel to women from below-poverty-line households. Reason (R): The scheme provides free LPG connections to eligible beneficiaries to reduce health risks caused by indoor air pollution.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The correct option is Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. Explanation: Assertion (A) is True. The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) is a flagship social welfare scheme of the Government of India. Its explicitly stated objective is to make clean cooking fuel, in the form of Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), available to women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) and other deprived households. Reason (R) is True. The mechanism of the scheme is to provide financial support to eligible beneficiaries that covers the cost of a new LPG connection, making it effectively free for them. The primary rationale behind this initiative is to safeguard the health of women and children. The use of traditional cooking fuels like firewood, coal, and cow-dung cakes leads to severe indoor air pollution, which is a major cause of respiratory illnesses and other health problems. R is the correct explanation of A. The Reason (R) perfectly explains the Assertion (A). The goal of providing clean cooking fuel (A) is achieved by the method of giving free connections, and it is motivated by the need to reduce health risks from indoor air pollution (R). Further Insights: The PMUY was launched on May 1, 2016. Besides the significant health benefits, the scheme also aims to empower women by reducing the drudgery of collecting firewood and to protect the environment by curbing deforestation. The scheme was later expanded under Ujjwala 2.0 to cover a wider range of beneficiaries who were left out of the initial phase.

questions.question: Which Supreme Court judgment struck down the 99th Constitutional Amendment and the NJAC Act as unconstitutional?
options: Kuldip Nayar v. Union of India (2006)
I.R. Coelho v. State of Tamil Nadu (2007)
Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association v. Union of India (2015)
S.P. Gupta v. Union of India (1981)
questions.explanation: In "Supreme Court Advocates-on-Record Association v. Union of India (2015)" (the "Fourth Judges Case"), a 5-judge Constitution Bench invalidated the 99th Amendment and the NJAC Act, holding that they violated the basic structure by undermining judicial independence and the primacy of the judiciary in appointments. Incorrect options: - "Kuldip Nayar v. Union of India (2006)": Upheld open ballot and domicile-neutral change for Rajya Sabha elections. - "I.R. Coelho v. State of Tamil Nadu (2007)": Held that Ninth Schedule laws are open to basic structure review if inserted after 24 April 1973. - "S.P. Gupta v. Union of India (1981)": First Judges Case; gave primacy to the executive in appointments (later overruled). Further Insights: - Second Judges Case (1993) established judicial primacy via the Collegium system. - Third Judges Case (1998, Presidential Reference) clarified Collegium composition and consultation. - The NJAC proposed a 6-member body including the Law Minister and eminent persons; struck down for diluting judicial primacy. - Post-2015, the Collegium continues with iterative reforms in the Memorandum of Procedure. - Judicial independence is part of the basic structure (Kesavananda Bharati, 1973). - The Court also discussed the "doctrine of separation of powers" in the context of appointments.

questions.question: India won its first Olympic medal at the 1900 Paris Olympics in which sport?
questions.explanation: Norman Pritchard won two silver medals in athletics (100m and 200m) at the 1900 Paris Olympics, earning India its first Olympic medals, though he competed for India. The International Olympic Committee (IOC) considers Norman Pritchard to be an Indian athlete and attributed both his Olympic medals to India. India first sent its official contingent of athletes at the 1920 Antwerp Olympics. The first-ever Indian Olympic contingent comprised Purma Bannerjee (100m and 400m), Phadeppa Chaugule (10000m and marathon), Sadashiv Datar (marathon), Kumar Navale and Dinkkarao Shinde (both wrestling).

questions.question: The Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04), successfully launched in June 2025, carried an Indian astronaut as its pilot. Who among the following was the Indian crew member on this mission?
questions.explanation: Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-04) was launched on 25 June 2025 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Kennedy Space Centre, Florida. The crew included Peggy Whitson (Commander), Shubhanshu Shukla (Pilot, India), Sławosz Uznański (Poland), and Tibor Kapu (Hungary). The launch vehicle and the Dragon spacecraft performed flawlessly, achieving all mission milestones-from stage separation to orbit insertion-well within expected parameters.

questions.question: Oryzenin is the major storage protein found in which of the following?
questions.explanation: Also called glutelin, oryzenin is the primary storage protein present in rice. Oryzenin is the major protein of rice. Glutelin (oryzenin) and prolamin (aqueous ethanol soluble) proteins constitute 80% and 2025% of rice endosperm protein, respectively. The name "oryzenin" itself is derived from "Oryza," which is the scientific genus name for rice (Oryza sativa). Other options: Barley: The major storage protein is hordein (a prolamin) Wheat: The major storage proteins are gliadins and glutenins (collectively called gluten) Maize: The major storage protein is zein (a prolamin)

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the "Break in the Monsoon" phenomenon in India?
options: A period of several weeks during the summer where no rainfall occurs across the entire Indian subcontinent.
The final withdrawal of the southwest monsoon from the Indian landmass starting from Rajasthan in September.
A temporary shift of the monsoon trough towards the Himalayan foothills, leading to heavy rain in the mountains and dry spells in the plains.
The arrival of moisture-laden winds from the Arabian Sea that causes the first heavy showers in Kerala.
questions.explanation: The "Break in the Monsoon" is a characteristic feature of the Indian summer monsoon season (June to September). It is not a complete cessation of rain but rather a distinct change in the rainfall pattern. During the active phase of the monsoon, the monsoon trough, a low-pressure belt, lies over the Indo-Gangetic plains, bringing widespread rain to this region. A "break" occurs when this monsoon trough shifts northwards, close to the foothills of the Himalayas. This shift causes the rainfall to become concentrated over the Himalayan foothills, the northeastern states, and the mountainous regions, often leading to heavy rains and flooding in these areas. Simultaneously, the vast plains of northern and central India experience a prolonged dry spell with very little or no rainfall.

questions.question: Which statement correctly characterizes mammalian mitochondrial ribosomes?
options: They are 80S and sensitive to cycloheximide like cytosolic ribosomes
They are 70S and identical to bacterial ribosomes in composition
They are ~55 - 60S with high protein:RNA ratio; mitochondrial translation is inhibited by chloramphenicol/tetracyclines
They are 60S and lack a small subunit, using cytosolic tRNAs
questions.explanation: Mammalian mitoribosomes are ~55 - 60S, with unusually high protein content relative to rRNA compared to bacterial 70S ribosomes. Their translation can be inhibited by antibiotics like chloramphenicol and tetracyclines, reflecting their bacterial ancestry and distinctiveness from cytosolic 80S ribosomes. Incorrect option explanation: - "A. They are 80S and sensitive to cycloheximide like cytosolic ribosomes:" 80S applies to cytosolic/eukaryotic ribosomes; mitoribosomes are ~55 - 60S; cycloheximide affects cytosolic, not mitochondrial, translation. - "B. They are 70S and identical to bacterial ribosomes in composition:" Not identical; mitoribosomes diverge substantially (more proteins, less rRNA) and differ in antibiotic sensitivities. - "D. They are 60S and lack a small subunit, using cytosolic tRNAs:" Mitoribosomes have distinct small and large subunits and use mitochondria-encoded and imported components; statement is incorrect. Further Insights: - Chloramphenicol blocks mitochondrial peptidyl transferase; aminoglycosides can affect mitochondrial translation and cause ototoxicity. - Cytochrome oxidase subunits (e.g., COX I - III) are mitochondrially encoded; nuclear genes encode most mitochondrial proteins. - Mitochondrial genetic code differs slightly (e.g., UGA codes Trp).

questions.question: In Indian classical music, "Gharanas" primarily refer to:
options: Regional schools of classical music with distinct styles
Musical competitions held in royal courts
questions.explanation: Gharanas in Indian classical music primarily refer to regional schools of classical music with distinct styles. A gharana is a system of social organization linking musicians by lineage or apprenticeship, and more importantly by adherence to a particular musical style. A gharana is a community of performers who share a distinctive musical style that traces to a particular instructor or region. The word "gharana" comes from the Hindi word "ghar" meaning "house" or "home," and while gharanas can be family-based, they represent much more than just family lineages. Gharanas typically refer to the place where the musical ideology originated, such as Gwalior, Delhi, Agra, Indore, Kirana, and Patiala for vocal music, and Lucknow, Jaipur, Benares, and Raigarh for Kathak dance. Each gharana has its own: -Distinctive musical style and approach to raga interpretation -Unique techniques and ornamentation -Specific teaching methodology (guru-shishya tradition) -Characteristic repertoire

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Plains: I. Plains are landforms with an extensive flat or gently undulating surface and usually lie below 300 metres above sea level. II. Floodplains are formed by rivers depositing sediments, which make the soil fertile and suitable for intensive agriculture. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Plains are landforms that have an extensive flat or gently undulating surface. They do not have any large hills or deep valleys. They are generally not more than 300 metres above sea level. Floodplains are one type of plains formed by rivers originating in mountain ranges, where they collect particles of rock, sand and silt called 'sediments'. These sediments are carried all the way to the plains, where the rivers deposit them, making the soil very fertile. As a result, these plains are ideal for growing crops of all kinds, and agriculture is a major economic occupation in this landform. Plains also support a variety of flora and fauna.

questions.question: "Fiscal Deficit" refers to:
options: Difference between total expenditure and total revenue (excluding borrowings)
questions.explanation: A fiscal deficit arises when a government's total expenditure surpasses its revenue, excluding any borrowed funds. The fiscal deficit can be calculated using the following formula: Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - Total Receipts (excluding borrowings). More specifically: Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - (Revenue Receipts + Non-debt Capital Receipts). Why the other options are incorrect: Total revenue minus capital expenditure - This is incorrect. Fiscal deficit considers TOTAL expenditure (both revenue and capital), not just capital expenditure. Borrowing by public sector banks - This is completely incorrect. Fiscal deficit is not about bank borrowing; it's about the government's budget shortfall. Net deficit after excluding subsidies - This is incorrect. Fiscal deficit does not exclude subsidies; it includes all government expenditures.

questions.question: What is the traditional uniform worn by practitioners of Taekwondo called?
questions.explanation: Taekwondo is a Korean martial art. The traditional uniform worn by Taekwondo practitioners is called a Dobok. A Dobok typically consists of: A jacket Pants A belt (to indicate rank) Gi is the general term used in Japanese martial arts like Judo and sometimes Karate. Karategi is the uniform worn in Karate. Hanbok is the traditional Korean dress, worn on cultural occasions. Further Insights Taekwondo originated in South Korea. The belt system in Taekwondo represents levels of skill. Beginners start at the 10th Geup (White Belt) and work their way up to 1st Geup (Red or Brown Belt). Advanced practitioners earn Dan ranks (Black Belt), which range from 1st to 9th or 10th Dan. Taekwondo is an Olympic sport since 2000. White Dobok symbolizes purity and discipline.

questions.question: Who has been appointed by the Central Government as the new Deputy Governor (DG) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), succeeding M. Rajeshwar Rao, effective from October 9, 2025?
questions.explanation: · Shirish Chandra Murmu was appointed as Deputy Governor of RBI, effective October 9, 2025, succeeding M. Rajeshwar Rao. · His appointment was made by the Central Government. · T. Rabi Sankar: He is already serving as one of the four Deputy Governors. He was originally appointed in 2021 and has received extensions · Poonam Gupta: becoming a Deputy Governor earlier in May 2025 (succeeding Michael Patra). · Swaminathan J.: He was appointed as a Deputy Governor in June 2023. He continues his tenure Further insights: · RBI can have up to four Deputy Governors at a time. · Deputy Governors are appointed by the Central Government under the RBI Act, 1934. · Each DG usually handles specific portfolios like regulation, supervision, payments, or monetary policy.

questions.question: The 1781 battle in which Eyre Coote checked Hyder Ali's advance toward Madras by a hard-fought coastal action is the Battle of ______.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Porto Novo" (1 July 1781), where Sir Eyre Coote halted Hyder Ali's momentum during the Second Anglo - Mysore War. The engagement showcased disciplined British infantry tactics against larger Mysorean forces, stabilizing the Coromandel front. - "Sholinghur": Another 1781 Coote victory, but inland, occurring later in the campaign. - "Pollilur": 1780 Mysorean victory over the British; not the check by Eyre Coote. - "Colachel": 1741 Travancore (Marthanda Varma) defeat of the Dutch; unrelated to the Anglo - Mysore War. Further Insights: - The Second Anglo - Mysore War ended with the Treaty of Mangalore (1784), restoring pre-war conditions (status quo ante). - Eyre Coote had earlier commanded at the Battle of Wandiwash (1760) against the French in the Seven Years' War Indian theatre. - Hyder Ali's alliance with the French complicated British coastal supply lines during the war. - The 1781 coastal engagements aimed to secure lines between Cuddalore and Madras. - Tipu Sultan continued the conflict after Hyder Ali's death in 1782 until the 1784 treaty.

questions.question: The "Procedure Established by Law" in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The phrase "procedure established by law" is found in Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, which states, "No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law." This concept was borrowed from the Constitution of Japan. It means that a law that is duly enacted by the legislature is valid, provided the correct procedure for its enactment has been followed. Under this doctrine, the judiciary's power of review is limited; it can only check whether the law has been made by a competent authority and according to the prescribed procedure, not whether the law itself is fair, just, and reasonable. Other options are incorrect: USA: The American Constitution uses the phrase "due process of law." This is a much wider concept that includes not only procedural checks but also a substantive check. It gives the courts the power to question the law itself and strike it down if it is found to be arbitrary, unfair, or unreasonable. Britain: The British system is based on the principle of Parliamentary Sovereignty, and concepts like rule of law and legislative procedure are central to its uncodified constitution. However, the specific phrase and its initial interpretation in the Indian context were taken from Japan. Canada: Key features borrowed from Canada include the quasi-federal structure, the distribution of powers, and the provision for a strong centre. Further Insights: Initially, the Indian Supreme Court interpreted Article 21 strictly according to the "procedure established by law" doctrine, as seen in the A.K. Gopalan case (1950). However, in the landmark Maneka Gandhi case (1978), the Supreme Court dramatically expanded the scope of Article 21. It ruled that the "procedure established by law" must also be "right, just, and fair" and not arbitrary, fanciful, or oppressive. This judgment effectively incorporated the American principle of "substantive due process" into the Indian constitutional framework, making Article 21 a powerful protector of individual liberties.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the 'Digital India' program?
questions.explanation: Housing programs fall under separate schemes like PM-Awas Yojana. In July 2015, the Indian government launched the 'Digital India' initiative to improve online infrastructure and increase internet accessibility among citizens (for example, linking rural areas to high-speed internet networks); thereby, empowering the country to become more digitally advanced. The initiative encompasses the following three key objectives: -Establish a secure and stable digital infrastructure -Deliver digital services -Ensure that every citizen has access to the Internet

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Mehal Pat festival: 1) Mehal Pat is a historic festival celebrated to commemorate the 16th-century meeting between Mughal Emperor Babur and Raja Nagpal of Bhadarwah. 2) The centerpiece of the festival is the display of the ancient royal "Pat" (robes) and the golden umbrella gifted by the Mughals. 3) A major highlight of Mehal Pat is the performance of the Dekhu dance, a traditional folk dance of the Bhadarwah region. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: While the meeting did take place in the 16th century, it was between Raja Nagpal and Emperor Akbar, not Babur. The "Patt" (exquisite silk robes) and the Chhatr (Golden Umbrella) are the sacred relics of the festival. They are brought out from the royal treasury and displayed to the public during the three-day event. The Dekhu dance is the soul of this festival. It is a rhythmic folk dance performed in a circle, where men carry the "Patt" structure, symbolizing the local culture and the victory of the Nag dynasty. Further insights It is exclusively celebrated in the Bhadarwah Valley (often called Chota Kashmir) in the Doda district of Jammu & Kashmir. Bhadarwah is known as "Chhota Kashmir" due to its landscape and climate.

questions.question: Match the following Economy Terms with their correct meanings: Column A (Economy Term) Column B (Meaning) 1. GDP A. GDP minus depreciation 2. GNP B. Value of all final goods & services produced by residents of a country (including abroad) 3. GVA C. Value added by all producers within the economy, before taxes on products 4. NDP D. Value of all final goods & services produced within a country in a given year
questions.explanation: GDP is the standard measure of the value of economic output produced within a country's geographical boundaries, regardless of who owns the means of production. GNP focuses on the output produced by the nationals or citizens of a country. It includes the income earned by its citizens from their activities abroad and excludes the income generated by foreign nationals within the country. It is calculated as GDP + Net Factor Income from Abroad. GVA measures the contribution to an economy of an individual producer, industry, or sector. It is the value of output minus the value of intermediate consumption. It provides a picture of the economy from the producers' or supply side. GDP is calculated from GVA as: GVA + Taxes on Products - Subsidies on Products. NDP is an adjusted measure of a country's economic output that accounts for the depreciation of capital goods (like machinery and buildings) that are used up during the production process. It is considered a more accurate measure of a country's actual production as it reflects the cost of maintaining the existing capital stock.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the Kharchi Puja festival of Tripura?
options: It is a harvest festival celebrated by the local tribes to honor the Goddess of Crops through traditional masked dances.
It is a week-long festival where fourteen deities representing the dynasty of the Tripura kings are worshipped in the temple of Agartala.
It is an annual boat racing festival held in the backwaters to commemorate the victory of local kings over sea invaders.
It is a quiet, meditative festival observed by the Buddhist community of the state to mark the birth and enlightenment of Lord Buddha.
questions.explanation: Kharchi Puja is one of the most important and popular festivals of the state of Tripura. The festival is centered around the worship of fourteen deities (the Chaturdasha Devata), who are the ancestral deities of the Tripuri people and the royal dynasty of Tripura. It takes place over a period of seven to ten days in the month of July at the Chaturdasha Devata Temple in Old Agartala. While it has tribal origins, the festival is now celebrated by all communities in Tripura, both tribal and non-tribal, and involves a large fair. The word 'Kharchi' is derived from 'Khya', which means "Earth," so it is also a celebration of the Earth. Further Insights: The fourteen deities include prominent Hindu gods like Shiva, Vishnu, Durga, and Lakshmi, as well as lesser-known deities, reflecting the syncretic nature of the region's religious traditions. A unique feature of the festival is that only the heads of the deities are worshipped. The festival begins with a procession where the idols of the fourteen deities are taken to the river Saidra for a holy bath, a ritual known as 'Snan Yatra'.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why the Western Ghats receive higher rainfall than Eastern Ghats?
options: They are lower in elevation, promoting vertical uplift
They run parallel to the Arabian Sea coastline, intercepting monsoon winds
They have higher forest cover and evapotranspiration
They are composed of basaltic lava rocks that absorb moisture
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats block rain-bearing winds which cause rainfall on the western slopes. Whereas South-west monsoon moves parallel to the Eastern Ghats, which cause less rainfall because in the Eastern Ghats unable to block moisture-laden winds. The Western Ghats play an important role in determining the climate and seasons in India. The air, which picks up moisture along the way and flows eastward from the Arabian Sea, is blocked by the Western Ghats. The rising air cools and brings about orographic precipitation along the western coast. Key reasons why Western Ghats receive more rainfall: 1.Orientation and positioning: The Western Ghats run almost perpendicular to the moisture-laden southwest monsoon winds coming from the Arabian Sea, acting as a natural barrier. 2.Orographic rainfall mechanism: The moisture laden monsoon winds passing over the Arabian Sea are obstructed by the Western Ghats and thus orographic rain in the windward side. Rainfall increases along the slopes of the WG and rapidly decreases on the eastern leeward side due to the sinking air. 3.Rain shadow effect: By the time the air rises above the mountains, it becomes dry, resulting in a rain shadow region with very little rainfall on the leeward side towards the interior of the Deccan plateau. Why the other options are incorrect: (a) Incorrect - The Western Ghats are actually HIGHER in elevation than the Eastern Ghats, not lower. (c) Incorrect - While forest cover exists, it's the result of high rainfall, not the cause. The primary reason is the orographic effect. (d) Incorrect - The rock composition doesn't absorb moisture; rather, the mountains force air upward, causing condensation and rainfall.

questions.question: Argon is commonly used in electric bulbs and welding because it:
questions.explanation: Argon is a noble gas with a complete outer electron shell, which makes it chemically inert. In electric bulbs, argon prevents the hot tungsten filament from reacting with oxygen and reduces evaporation of the filament. In welding, argon acts as a shielding gas, protecting molten metal from reacting with oxygen, nitrogen, or moisture in the air.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Dravidian temple architecture: I. The Vimana is the main temple tower, which increases in size in a stepped pyramid fashion. II. The Gopuram serves as the monumental entrance gateway, often overshadowing the main shrine in height in later periods. III. The Mandapa is a pillared hall used for public rituals and is always detached from the main sanctum. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements I and II are correct. Statement III is incorrect because while a Mandapa is a pillared hall, it is not always detached; it is often connected to the main sanctum. Explanation of Statements: I. is Correct. The Vimana is one of the most distinctive features of Dravidian temple architecture. It is the towering superstructure built directly over the garbhagriha (the sanctum sanctorum where the main deity is housed). It is characteristically shaped like a stepped pyramid, rising in multiple stories (talas), with each story being smaller than the one below it. II. is Correct. The Gopuram is the monumental, ornate tower that serves as the entrance gateway to the temple complex. In early Dravidian temples, the Vimana was the most prominent and tallest structure. However, in the later periods (especially from the Pandya, Vijayanagara, and Nayaka periods onwards), the Gopurams became progressively larger and more elaborate, often dwarfing the main Vimana in height and scale. The Meenakshi Temple in Madurai is a prime example of this trend. III. is Incorrect. A Mandapa is indeed a pillared hall or pavilion used for public rituals, assemblies, marriages, and religious discourses. However, the statement that it is "always detached" is false. Temple complexes often feature multiple mandapas. Some, like the mahamandapa (great hall), might be detached, but others, like the ardhamandapa (antechamber), are directly attached to and lead into the main sanctum. Further Insights: Dravidian temple architecture is one of the three main styles of Hindu temple architecture, primarily found in Southern India. Other key features of this style include a compound wall (prakara) enclosing the entire temple complex and a large temple tank or water reservoir (kalyani or teppakulam). The Brihadeshwara Temple in Thanjavur is a classic example of an early Chola temple where the Vimana is the tallest and most dominant structure, in contrast to later temples where the Gopurams became the focal point.

questions.question: Which of the following metal - purification method pairs is NOT correctly matched?
options: Van Arkel method - Titanium and Zirconium
Parkes process - Removal of zinc from lead
questions.explanation: The Parkes process removes silver from lead by adding zinc; silver preferentially dissolves in molten zinc forming a crust (dross) separable from lead. It is not for removing zinc from lead. The other pairs are standard: Mond for Ni, Van Arkel for Ti/Zr, and zone refining for ultrapure semiconductors. Incorrect options: - A: Mond process forms volatile Ni(CO)4 at low temperature, decomposed to give pure Ni. - B: Van Arkel (iodide) process purifies Ti/Zr via volatile metal iodides that decompose on a hot filament. - C: Zone refining exploits differential solubility of impurities in molten vs solid phases (e.g., Si, Ge). Further Insights: - Liquation is used for purifying low-melting metals like tin and lead from higher-melting impurities. - Distillation purifies metals with low boiling points (e.g., zinc, mercury). - Electrorefining is widely used for copper purification (anode slime yields precious metals).

questions.question: If the five-digit number 886xy is divisible by 3 and11, then what is the value of (8x + 5y)?
questions.explanation: 886xy For checking divisibility test of 3 = When sum of digits of number should be divisible by 3. 8 + 8 + 6 + x + y = 22 + x + y, x + y = 2, 5, 8, 11 etc. For checking divisibility test of 11 = Difference of sum of alternative terms should be 0 or multiple of 11. (8 + 6 + y) - (8 + x) = 0 or 11 (14 + y) - (8 + x) = 0 or 11 If we take y = 8 or x = 3 (14 + 8) - (8 + 3) = 0 or 11 (22 - 11) = 11 Therefore, the value of x = 3 and y = 8 (8x + 5y) = (8 X 3 + 5 X 8) = 64

questions.question: A thief steals a bike at 2:00 am and drives it at the speed of 46km/hr. At 3:00 am the thief is discovered, and the owner starts chasing him with another bike at the speed of 69 km/hr. At what time owner be able to catch the thief?
questions.explanation: Time after which thief will be caught = Distance / Relative speed of owner and thief = 46 / 23 = 2 hr. The owner will be able to catch the thief on (3 + 2) = 5:00 am.

questions.question: The perimeter of a sector of a circle is 73 cm and the radius is 9 cm. Calculate the area (in sq. cm) of the sector.
questions.explanation: Arc length = Perimeter of sector - Diameter = 73 - 18 = 55 cm Area of sector = (½) * arc length * radius = (½) * (55) * 9 =247.5 sq. cm

questions.question: Out of 750 students of classes X, XI and XII of a school, 24% are in class X and 40% of the remaining are in class XII. If 50% of the number of students in class XI are girls, then find the number of boys in class XI.
questions.explanation: Total number of students = 750 Students in class XI = (100 - 40)% of (100 - 24)% of 750 = 342 Boys in class XI = 50% of 342 = 171

questions.question: The speed of a boat in downstream and upstream are 17 km/hr and 10 km/hr respectively. Find the time taken by the boat to cover 297 km moving in still water.
questions.explanation: Downstream speed = Speed of the boat in still water + Speed of the stream = 17 km/hr Upstream speed = Speed of the boat in still water - Speed of the stream = 10 km/hr Speed of the boat in still water = (17 + 10)/2 = 27/2 km/hr Required time = 297/(27/2) = 22 hours

questions.question: One chord of a circle is given as 38.8 cm, then the radius (r) of the circle must be -
questions.explanation: One chord of a circle = 38.8 cm Since, diameter of a circle is the longest chord so it must be equal to or more than the chord of a circle. d ≥ 38.8 r ≥ 19.4

questions.question: A shopkeeper sold two articles at Rs. 900 each. If the shopkeeper has a gain of 12.5% on the first article and a loss of 25% on the other article, then the gain or loss percentage on selling both articles is:
questions.explanation: C.P of 1ˢᵗ article = 900 * 100/(100 + 12.5) = Rs. 800 C.P of 2ⁿᵈ article = 900 * 100/ (100 - 25) = Rs. 1200 C.P. of both articles = 800 + 1200 = Rs. 2000 S.P. of both articles = 900 * 2 = 1800 Loss = 2000 - 1800 = Rs. 200 Hence, the required loss% = 200 * 100/2000 = 10%

questions.question: A path of uniform width surrounds a circular park. The difference of internal and external circumferences of this circular path is 132 m. Its width is [take ℼ = 22/7]
questions.explanation: "Let the internal radius of the park berand the external radius (with the path) be R. Now, according to the question, Difference of circumference = 132 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/a6ba8f667e6cea715858a15f85591405.png) Hence, the width of path = 21m "

questions.question: Following table shows the number of movies screened by theatre X and theatre Y in various months. MonthsXY August12001400 September900650 October1150800 What is the total number of movies screened by X in September and number of movies screened by Y in August and October together?
questions.explanation: Number of movies screened by X in September = 900 Number of movies screened by Y in August and October together = 1400 + 800 = 2200 Required total = 900 + 2200 = 3100

questions.question: Which of the following line is parallel to 7x + 15y = 17?
questions.explanation: Equation of line => 7x + 15y - 17 = 0 For parallel lines - a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂ Option a: 14x + 15y - 17 = 0 7/14 = 15/15 ≠ -17/-17½ = 1 ≠ 1 (false) Option b: 7x + 15y + 13 = 0 7/7 = 15/15 ≠ -17/13 1 = 1 ≠ -17/13 (true) Option c: 7x + 30y - 10 = 0 7/7 = 15/30 ≠ -17/-10 1 = ½ ≠ 17/10 (false) Option d: 14x + 30y - 34 = 0 7/14 = 15/30 ≠ -17/-34½ = ½ ≠ ½ (false) Hence, option 'b' is correct.

questions.question: Isha spends 28% of her salary on housing, 19% on debt, 34% on transportation and 8% on other expenses. If she saves ₹8294, then find the amount she spends on debt.
questions.explanation: Let total amount = 100x Savings of Isha = ₹8294 Amount she spends on debt = 19 * 8294/(100 - 28 - 19 - 34 - 8) = 19*8294/11 = ₹14326

questions.question: A storm comes and a tree breaks. The broken part bends so that the top of the tree makes an angle of 30° with the ground. Find the height of the tree if the distance between the foot of the tree and the point where the top touches the ground is 17 metres. (In m)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1167/image004.jpg) tan30 = AB/BC => AB = BC * tan30 = 17/√3 cos30 = BC/AC => AC = BC/cos30 = 34/√3 Height = AB + AC = (17/√3) + (34/√3) = 51/√3 = 17√3

questions.question: If simple interest earned on a sum of Rs. B at r% per annum for t years is Rs. A, and simple interest earned on the sum of Rs. C at the same rate of interest and the same time period is Rs. B, then which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: A = (B * r * t)/100 => A/B = rt/100 And B = (C * r * t)/100 => B/C = rt/100 So, A/B = B/C => AC = B²

questions.question: Two circles A and B have same center and the radius of circle A is 5 cm more than the radius of circle B. A chord of the larger circle is a tangent of the smaller circle. If length of the chord is 24 cm, then find the radius of the larger circle.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19481/image001.jpg) According to the question: Here, let radius of circle A = x cm Radius of circle B = (x - 5) cm Length of chord = 24 cm Using Pythagoras theorem, we get (24/2)² + (x - 5)² = x² 144 + 25 + x² - 10x = x² 10x = 169 x = 16.9 cm

questions.question: P and Q started a business by investing a total of Rs. 7350. At the end of the year, out of the total profit of Rs. 3220, Q received Rs. 920 as her share of the profit. What was the amount that P invested in the business?
questions.explanation: Ratio of the share of profit received by Q out of total profit = 920:3220 = 2:7 So, ratio of investment by P out of total investment = (7 - 2):7 = 5:7 Hence, investment by P = (5/7) * 7350 = Rs. 5250

questions.question: A shopkeeper gives two successive discounts of x% and 15% respectively at the marked price of an article. The total discount given by shopkeeper is 23.5% of the marked price of article. If the marked price of article is Rs.2400, then what is the first amount of discount offered by the shopkeeper?
questions.explanation: Given that, The marked price of article = Rs.2400 Selling price of the item after two successive discounts = (100 - x)% of 85% of 2400 = (100 - 23.5)% of 2400 85(100 - x) = 76.5 * 100 100 - x = 90 x = 10 First amount of discount offered = 10% of 2400 = Rs.240 Alternate method: x + 15 - [(15 * x)/100] = 23.5 (15 + x) - 0.15x = 23.5 0.85x = 8.5 x = 10 First amount of discount offered = 10% of 2400 = Rs.240

questions.question: If √7 = 2.645 and √11 = 3.316, then what is the value of 2/(√11 + √7)?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27581/image015.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27581/image016.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27581/image017.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27581/image018.png) = 0.671/2 = 0.3355

questions.question: The base of a pyramid is a square with side 20 cm. If the height of the pyramid is 15 cm, find the lateral surface area of the pyramid.
questions.explanation: Square side = 20 cm => half-side = 10 cm. Slant height l = √(10² + 15²) = √(100 + 225) = √325 = 5√13 cm. Perimeter = 4 * 20 = 80 cm LSA = ¹/₂ * perimeter * slant = ¹/₂ * 80 * 5√13 = 200√13 cm².

questions.question: The average height of 32 students is 150 cm and the average height of 17 students out of 32 is 156 cm. Find the average height of remaining students.
questions.explanation: Let the average height of remaining students = H cm According to the question: 32*150 = 17*156 + (32 - 17)*H 4800 = 2652 + 15H 15H = 2148 H = 143.2 cm

questions.question: Simplify the following expression: (2a - 3b)³ + 6ab (6a - 9b).
questions.explanation: (2a - 3b)³ + 6ab (6a - 9b) = 8a³ - 27b³ - 18ab (2a - 3b) + 36a²b - 54ab² = 8a³ - 27b³ - 36a²b + 54ab² + 36a²b - 54ab² = 8a³ - 27b³

questions.question: Three vessels A, B and C contain mixture of milk and water in the ratio of 3: 1, 4: 1 and 5: 1 respectively. If 12 liters, 30 liters and 36 liters of mixture from vessels A, B and C respectively taken out and poured into another vessel D, then what is amount of milk in vessel D?
questions.explanation: Amount of milk taken out from vessel A = 12 * ¾ = 9 liters Amount of milk taken out from vessel B = 30 * ⅘ = 24 liters Amount of milk taken out from vessel C = 36 * ⅚ = 30 liters So, amount of milk in vessel D = 9 + 24 + 30 = 63 liters

questions.question: Which option with given sides forms a triangle?
questions.explanation: We know that sum of any two sides of a triangle is always greater than the third side. From option a, 10 + 20 = 30 (not satisfying condition) From option b, 18 + 3 = 21 < 22 (not satisfying condition) From option c, 14 + 6 = 20 (not satisfying condition) From option d, 28 + 26 = 54 > 29 28 + 29 = 57 > 26 26 + 29 = 55 > 28 Satisfying the conditions.

questions.question: A rectangular sheet of length 40 cm is cut from a square sheet of perimeter 240 cm. If the area of remaining sheet is 2400 cm², then find the breadth of the rectangular sheet.
questions.explanation: Let width of the rectangle = 'b' cm Perimeter of square sheet = 240 cm Area of square sheet = (240/4)² = 3600 cm² Area of remaining sheet = 2400 cm² Area of square sheet - area of rectangular sheet = 2400 3600 - 40*b = 2400 40b = 1200 b = 30 cm

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 9 cm is melted completely and recast into 27 identical cubes. What is the edge length of each cube?
questions.explanation: Volume of sphere = 4/3πr³ = 4/3 * π * 9³ = 972π cm³ Each cube's volume = 972π/27 = 36π cm³ Edge length of each cube = ∛(36π) cm

questions.question: The difference and the product of two numbers are 32 and 2145 respectively. Their sum is:
questions.explanation: Let two numbers be a,b a-b = 32 ; ab=2145 (a+b)² = (a-b)²+4ab = 32 X 32+4 X 2145 =9604 Then a+b = 98

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "ENERVATE".
questions.explanation: The word "Enervate" means to weaken or drain energy. 1) "Invigorate" - opposite; to energize or strengthen. 2) "Debilitate" - correct synonym; it also means to make weak or feeble. 3) "Fortify" - opposite; to strengthen or reinforce. 4) "Galvanize" - unrelated; means to stimulate into action. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The ruler or a person who is a tyrant
questions.explanation: Despot is a cruel and oppressive dictator Anarchist is an advocate of anarchism i.e. a political theory favouring the abolition of governments Righteous is characterized by accepted standards of morality or justice Saviour a person who rescues you from harm or danger A is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Word Meaning Woundan injury to living tissue caused by a cut, blow, or another impact, typically one in which the skin is cut or broken. Severevery great; intense. Associatedconnect (someone or something) with something else in one's mind. Mortifiedto feel very embarrassed or ashamed. The incorrect spelling is 'asociated'. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. Mrs. Arora has got the green light from the principal to organise a science conference in the college.
options: Got permission to go ahead with something
Got energy and motivation to do something
questions.explanation: The idiom 'get the green light' means to receive permission to ahead with something. Hence, b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The banners ________ by the protesters.
questions.explanation: The blank requires a verb to be relatable with the plural noun 'banners' and to match the Passive voice of the sentence. 'Is removed' and 'was removed' correspond to singular nouns and 'are removing' is a verb in Active voice format so they are negated. 'Were removed' is the most appropriate form of the verb that can be used in the Passive voice format of the sentence and also matches the context of the given sentence. The sentence is in the passive voice as the action 'removed' is directed at the subject 'banners'. Hence, the correct answer is option (d). The correct sentence is: The banners were removed by the protesters. Note: Active and Passive Voice: When the person doing the action is the subject, the sentence is said to be in active form/voice. When the subject is what the action is directed at, then the sentence is said to be in passive form/voice. For example: Active Voice: Rita and her friends performed a skit. Here 'Rita and her friends' (subject) perform the action. Passive Voice: A skit was performed by Rita and her friends. Here 'skit' is the subject (at which the action is directed).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The brave knight embarked on a _____ quest to retrieve the enchanted artefact.
questions.explanation: 'Perilous' means full of danger or risk. It perfectly aligns with the context of a brave knight embarking on a quest to retrieve an enchanted artifact. The word "perilous" emphasizes the challenging and hazardous nature of the journey, making it the correct answer. The complete sentence is: The brave knight embarked on a perilous quest to retrieve the enchanted artefact. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The rest of the options don't fit in contextually. Flowery - covered with flowers or having a floral design.

questions.question: Find the wrongly spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option a) is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling should be 'bizarre (strange)'. 'Fiery' means 'quick to become angry'. 'Allegiance' means 'support for a leader, government, belief, etc.' Hence, option a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Let the file be submitted by tomorrow.
options: It is requested that you submit the file by tomorrow.
Please submit the file by tomorrow.
Tomorrow the file should be submitted.
questions.explanation: ·The correct answer is (b) Submit the file by tomorrow. ·The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into subject of the Active Voice. ·The removal of the helping verb ['be' in this case] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. ·Since the sentence is in imperative form in passive voice, it should be an imperative sentence in Active voice too. ·Imperative sentence is used to express order, request, suggestion, etc. ·In Passive voice, the sentence begins with "Let". ·Here, the imperative sentence in Active voice is an order. ·The format in Passive voice is: Let + Subject (Objective case) + be + V3. ·Thus, the format in Active voice is: V1 + Object. ·For example: o Let the match be started. (Passive) o Start the match. (Active) ·The options except (b) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. ·Therefore, the correct answer is: Submit the file by tomorrow.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "EXASPERATE".
questions.explanation: The word "Exasperate" means to intensely irritate or provoke. 1) "Mollify" - opposite; to calm or soothe. 2) "Infuriate" - correct synonym; to make extremely angry. 3) "Appease" - opposite; to pacify. 4) "Assuage" - opposite; to relieve. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: The compound was surrounded by high walls.
options: The chemist mixed the chemicals within the compound of the lab, carefully.
The prison compound held maximum-security inmates.
She created a new compound word for her poem.
The factory compound included warehouses and offices.
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, "compound" is a noun meaning an enclosed area. Options a), b) and d) use "compound" in the same sense. Option c) uses "compound" as a chemical mixture, which is unrelated to space. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
options: The team are winning the match easily.
The team were winning the match easily.
The team is winning the match easily.
The team be winning the match easily.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is (c). Collective noun "team" takes singular verb "is" when acting as a unit (e.g., winning together). Option (a) is incorrect because "are" treats "team" as plural individuals, but context implies unity. Option (b) is incorrect as "were" is past tense; present continuous fits ongoing action. Option (d) is incorrect because "be" lacks auxiliary for continuous tense; it needs "is/are." Hence, the correct answer is option (c). Note: Collective Noun: It is the name given to a number (or collection) of persons or things taken together and spoken of as one whole. Examples could be crowd, fleet, committee, family, army, jury, heard, etc.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Most people were polite,/ but we did found that older people/ were more polite/ than younger people.
options: but we did found that older people
questions.explanation: In any sentence, 'do/does/did' is always followed by a base form of the verb. So, the use of 'found' (past simple (V1)/past participle (V3) form of the verb) in the given sentence is incorrect. It should be replaced with the base verb 'find'. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Parents in the US and UK are advisable to have their babies sleep in the same room as them for at least the first six months.
questions.explanation: The use of the adjective "advisable" is incorrect because it renders the sentence meaningless. Option b) is eliminated because the use of the preposition "for" renders the phrase and thereby, the sentence meaningless. Option c) is grammatically incorrect; "were" must be followed by the participle form of the verb (but "allow" is the base form of the verb). Option a) is grammatically correct and gives a meaningful sentence. Hence, a) is the correct answer. NOTE/- Participle: A word formed from a verb (e.g. going, gone, being, been) and used as an adjective (e.g. working woman, burnt toast) or a noun (e.g. good breeding). In English participles are also used to make compound verb forms (e.g. is going, has been). Consider the sentences given below: Dancing at the party, the women fell on the ground. Encouraged by her parents, Rita went on to pursue her passion. The highlighted word in the first sentence is of the form Verb + ing and is used as a present participle. It is used to denote an action that is going on or incomplete or imperfect. The highlighted word in the second sentence is of the form verb + ed. It is used as a past participle. It denotes completed action or state of the thing spoken of. A past participle ends with the following: -ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n. One should take great care and never get confused between a gerund and present participle. They use the same forms but perform different functions: a gerund is a verbal noun, while a present participle is a verbal adjective.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. One can never imagine / how hot it gets in Delhi / unless one doesn't go/ there in person.
options: How hot it gets in Delhi
questions.explanation: We use until as a subordinating conjunction to connect an action or an event to a point in time. Example: Let's wait here until the rain stops. So, 'unless' should be replaced with 'until'. Hence, option (b) is the answer. 'Unless' is similar in meaning to 'if not' and can be used instead of 'if not' in certain types of conditional sentences. The correct sentence is: One can never imagine how hot it gets in Delhi until one doesn't go there in person.

questions.question: Choose the word closest in meaning to "elusive" as used in the passage: "When existence is examined more closely, stability itself appears elusive."
questions.explanation: Here, "elusive" refers to something that is hard to grasp, define, or hold on to, in this case stability. Among the options, "difficult to define" best conveys this meaning. The other options suggest clarity, permanence, or ease, which are opposite in sense. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses the central idea of the passage?
options: Change is always linear and depends on identifiable starting points
Human perception imposes beginnings on change, which is fundamentally continuous
Philosophical reflection shows that discrete events cannot explain the concept of change
Stability and change are separate phenomena, with beginnings marking their boundary
questions.explanation: The passage explains that although humans perceive change as starting from definite points, philosophical reflection shows change as continuous. Beginnings are described as conceptual markers, not absolute realities. Therefore, option (b) best captures the central idea. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best reflects the author's attitude towards human understanding of change?
options: Analytical and reflective, highlighting perceptual limitations
Prescriptive, recommending a strict sequence for understanding events
Dismissive, portraying human perception as irrelevant to reality
Narrative and anecdotal, relying on historical examples to explain change
questions.explanation: The author systematically analyses how humans perceive change and beginnings, reflecting on cognitive frameworks and perceptual limitations. The tone is analytical and reflective, not prescriptive, dismissive, or anecdotal. Therefore, option (a) best reflects the author's attitude. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What grammatical function does the phrase "rather than as absolute starting points" serve in the sentence below? "Apparent beginnings function as conceptual markers that help humans make sense of continuous movement rather than as absolute starting points."
options: It provides a contrasting idea to the preceding clause
It describes the subject of the main clause
It functions as an adverb modifying the main verb
questions.explanation: The phrase "rather than as absolute starting points" contrasts what the conceptual markers do with what they are not. It does not describe the subject, modify the main verb, or indicate condition, but introduces contrast within the clause. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, which of the following is cited as an example of how change is commonly perceived in everyday life?
options: Ageing, decay, and adaptation as continuous processes
Growth beginning with birth and learning beginning with instruction
Stability being elusive in philosophical reflection
Conceptual beginnings functioning as narrative markers
questions.explanation: The passage states: "In everyday life, change is frequently associated with observable starting points. Growth appears to begin with birth, learning with instruction, and transformation with a triggering event." This line explicitly provides examples of how people perceive change as having identifiable beginnings. The other options refer to philosophical perspectives or abstract ideas, not everyday perceptions. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Raveen told Neha that he had been thinking about their wedding.
options: Raveen said to Neha, "I am thinking about our wedding."
Raveen said to Neha, "We have been thinking about my wedding."
Raveen said to Neha, "I thought about our wedding."
Raveen said to Neha, "I have been thinking about our wedding."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will be in Present Perfect Continuous Tense Past Perfect Continuous (Subject + had been + Present Participle + Object) will be converted into Present Perfect Continuous (Subject + has/have been + Present participle + Object). For example: He said that they had been waiting for hours. (Indirect Speech) He said, "We have been waiting for hours" (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: · The reporting verb 'told' is changed into 'said to'. · The pronoun 'he' is changed into 'I'. · The possessive determiner 'their' is changed into 'our'. · The verb 'had been thinking' (past perfect continuous) is changed into 'have been thinking' (present perfect continuous). All options except (d) don't follow these changes. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Therefore, the correct answer is: Raveen said to Neha, "I have been thinking about our wedding."

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. (a) you will, of course, be able to demonstrate (b) verbal communication skills (c) have excellent written and (d) strong commercial skills, entrepreneurial flare and
questions.explanation: 'a' is the introductory part of the sentence as it has the subject (you). 'd' talks about the various skills that need to be demonstrated, and those skills are continued in 'c' and 'b'. 'And' at the end of part 'c' implies that part 'b' will come after part 'c'. The correct sequence of the parts of the sentence is a,d,c,b. Hence, option (b) is correct. The sentence is - "You will, of course, be able to demonstrate strong commercial skills, entrepreneurial flare and have excellent written and verbal communication skills."

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. A salt seller used to carry the salt bag on his donkey to the market every day. B. One day the donkey suddenly tumbled down the stream and the salt bag also fell into the water. C. On the way they had to cross a stream. D. The salt dissolved in the water and hence the bag became very light to carry.
questions.explanation: Statement A is the opening sentence as it introduces the donkey, the salt seller and the salt bag which are then referred to in the other statements. Statement D is the outcome of statement B hence D follows statement B; statement C explains the path they follow and mentions the stream in their way; as mentioned in statement B, the bag fell in the same stream; hence statement C should precede statement B. The correct order is ACBD. Hence, option b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Cognizant
questions.explanation: 'Cognizant' means having knowledge or awareness. 'Oblivious' means to be unaware, inattentive, or ignorant of something. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Mindful- means being attentive, aware, and conscious of the present moment. Apprehensive- a feeling of anxiety, fear, or unease about something that may happen in the future. Cautious- a state of being careful, prudent, or taking preventative measures to avoid risks or potential harm.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Castigate
questions.explanation: Castigate means to reprimand (someone) severely. For example, "The coach had to castigate the players for their poor performance." Laud means to praise (a person or their achievements) highly, especially in a public context. For example, "The author was lauded for her groundbreaking novel." High praise is the direct opposite of severe criticism, making laud the correct antonym. The other options - Reprimand, Chastise, and Rebuke are all synonyms of castigate. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person recovering from an illness or medical treatment
questions.explanation: Cynosure - the centre of attention. Crusade - a war or campaign for religious, political or social change. Convalescent - a person recovering from a disease or medical treatment. Almanac - an annual calendar containing important dates and statistical information. Hence, option (c) is the answer.
